rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ODYSSEE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The petition was verified by the attorney and not by the personal representative because the personal representative was absent from the county. Probate Code 1023 disallows an attorney from verifying a pleading when the client is a fiduciary appointed in this proceeding. 2) Cash in the estate has not been, and is currently not, fully FDIC insured. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF WILLIAM ESTRADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/23/23 in anticipation of an amended accounting petition on file two weeks prior for an examiner's review of amened accounting petition. Currently "Petition for termination of Conservatorship of the Estate of William Estrada, Conservatee" (filed 4/18/2023) is before the court on 7/12/23. This petition merely prays termination of conservatorship. Even though the conservator filed additional document [titled as final report] on 6/30/2023; the examiner is unable to review this final report as it does not pertain to the 4/18/23 petition in its caption/title. Petitioner should have paid filing fee and obtained a new hearing date or its caption should have clarified its nature. CRC 7.3. Amended petition should set forth all relief in caption pursuant to CRC 7.102 and should be statutorily compliant, including but limited to aver all allegations under PC 1064; all schedules under PC 1062 and 1063; to cover two appropriate accounting periods (first, from date of appointment to date of death and then up to current) under PC 2620; to include all required statements. PC 2620(c); to show why and how the proposed distribution can be 100% to a niece; to have notice of hearing. CRC 7.53; to set forth who is entitled to notice; proposed order and courtesy to be submitted before hearing. LRSF 14.7.A and B. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT VEAL JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Decedent's will states that the "net profits from all rental properties can be divided equally", but it does not state to whom the profits should be distributed to. Petitioner alleges that they should be distributed to only the children of the Decedent. 2) The will states that a trust must be set up so that all children in the family can finish college. Petitioner alleges that all interested parties have waived their right to such funds. 3) $200,000 in distributions were made without prior court approval. 4) Cash in the estate was not fully insured and were not placed into interest bearing accounts. 5) Bryan Veal, co-administrator, was paid $2,250 per month to manage estate real properties. 6) Administrators paid litigation attorneys over $48,000 to represent the estate without court authority, despite Local Rule 14.50. 7) The signatures of Asya Veal, Isaiah Veal, and Roni Veal on the "Approval of Amended Petition for Final Distribution" filed on 5/16/23 are not ink signatures (they appear to be computer fonts). Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARITA REYES GENERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/23. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The attorney's amended declaration filed on 5/11/23 has just one bank statement attached to it, verifying just $250,000.27 on hand as of 5/10/23. (2) The supporting schedules must be detailed. [Probate Code Section 1062(b). Directing the Court to "See Exhibit A" is not sufficient. Furthermore, the costs shown on that Exhibit A (seller's closing statement) do not appear to agree with the lump sum shown on the disbursements schedule, and the filing fee for this petition was $435.00. (3) The assignment made on 2/18/22 and filed on provides for the full amount ($45,000.00) to be distributed to the assignee, who will then refund a portion if distribution is made within certain date parameters. Under what authority can the Court reduce the distribution to the assignee? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Bond
rulings.Rulings: This compliance hearing (on status of filing bond) is continued to 7/26/2023. The court is unable to determine on properness of bond as the Trustee has failed to make a request for proper bond amount for the court to determine despite the court ordered on 4/7/2023. Conservator/Trustee has failed to comply with the court order entered on 4/7/2023 on multiple issues as follow: (1) Filing final Inventory and Appraisal (hearing continued to 7/26/2023) (2) Filing a signed copy of the Trust in a separated Trust case (PTR case number) (hearing continue to 7/26/2023) (3) Filing bond (hearing continued to 7/26/2023) (4) Failed to appear at the hearing on 6/28/2023 (Order to appear on 7/26/2023) (5) Filing ex-parte petition for the court to determine proper bond amount in a separated Trust case (PTR case number) (should be filed immediately) Appears the case has not reported any progress since the court approved Substituted Judgment Petition on 4/5/2023. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSE CHIHHSI CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/15/23. The following issues to be addressed via supplemental declaration verified by the petitioner: 1) No allegation was made as to whether any action was taken on rejected claim. 2) Please confirm that all income taxes payable have been paid. 3) Petitioner alleges that additional income taxes for 2023 will be payable. A reserve should be requested for the payment of those taxes. 4) The petition at par. 17 states "Chun Yen Chen hereby ratifies the actions of the Administrator to having the real property transferred to her". Please clarify what is meant by this and whether the real property has already been transferred. In addition, the following must be filed / provided: 1) Notice of Hearing on the proper Judicial Council Form. 2) Proposed order (which should also be e-lodged, via e-filing). 3) Response to objection. All required documents to be filed and provided by 7/31/23.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BENG SAN ONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) No corrected proof of service has been filed yet. The one filed on 2/28/23 was verified on "2/2/2/23", BEFORE notice was mailed on 2/28/23 (without the remote appearance procedures attached). (3) There was no election by the surviving spouse attached to the petition, as alleged in paragraph 15 of the petition. Deadline is 9/8/23 for delivering the endorsed filed courtesy copy of the supplement to the petition and the correct proof of service of the complete Notice of Hearing to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MALINAO FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For 1. Order Compelling Trustee To Account 2. Redress Of Trustee Breaches 3. Suspension Of Trustee 4. Removal Of Trustee 5. Disallowance Of Compensation 6. Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/23/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to both counsel on 05/23/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received from the petitioner. 2) No proposed order has been received from the petitioner. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 4) Direct notice must be given to the respondent trustee. CRC 7.51(a)(2). 5) Did Andres Malinao Jr. predecease Socorro Malinao? If so, please provide the date of death and identify all descendants (names, addresses, and ages if minors). 6) Did Jane Gardiola predecease Socorro Malinao? If so, please provide the date of death and identify all descendants (names, addresses, and ages if minors). If either beneficiary survived Socorro Malinao, the personal representative of the beneficiary's estate must be identified and receive notice. If no probate administration is pending or anticipated, an allegation must be made as to whether the beneficiary died intestate or testate, and each person entitled to notice under Probate Code section 17203(c) must be identified and receive notice. If any descendant is a minor, all persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor (both parents if applicable) must be identified and receive a separate copy of notice under CRC 7.51(d).

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition By Windy R. Cherry Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Declaratory Relief/Damages: 1. Determining Existence And Validity Of Trust Provisions And Amendments And Compelling Trustee To Account And Provide Financial Infomration Concerning Trust Property And Compelling Redress Of A Breach Of The Trust; 3. Removal Of Trustee And Appointment Of Successor Or Independent Trustee; 4. Breach Of Trust; 5. Deeming Respondents To Have Predeceased Settlor; And 6. For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/23 for the following issues: (1) A verified supplement to petition needs to be filed two weeks prior, identifying who Jennifer, Christopher and Kimberly are. Notice of hearing was sent to each of them; however, Attachment 14 does not list them. If they are entitled to notice, notice of directions for remote appearances and access should be given to them. LRSF 4.1.D.6). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET CAITLIN WESTERFIELD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities must be filed. (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) Is the decedent's name Caitlin Margaret Westerfield or Margaret Caitlin Westerfield? Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/11/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
